Originally founded in 2014 in Leipers Fork, TN., GratiDude Ranch recently opened a second location in Northern California in a charming town called Nevada City. GratiDude Ranch offers a mini-farm, private resort vacation experience. Both locations support agritourism by offering short-term overnight accommodations, private farm dinner chef experiences, murder mystery dinner events and popular learning Birthday Bash gatherings. Private and special events can also be booked. A portion of proceeds from all events help support foster children and foster families through SAFE, a 501c3 non-profit.

decisions.name: Palisades Tahoe Lodge
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: Palisades Tahoe Lodge Rentals condominiums are your convenient and relaxing home base for year-round Tahoe vacation value! Step into adventure and unwind in style with our upscale, ski-in/ski-out vacation rentals. These units are also conveniently located at the Olympic Valley base of Palisades Tahoe. Our privately owned condominiums blend mountain luxury with laid-back comfort… perfect for a four-season escape. Whether you’re carving fresh powder in the winter or exploring scenic trails in the summer, you’ll enjoy unbeatable access to world-class outdoor activities, festivals, and Lake Tahoe’s natural beauty. decisions.name: 1882 Bar & Grill at The River Street Inn
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: The River Street Inn offers small Hotel charm in historic Old Town Truckee. A perfect vacation getaway, just minutes from Lake Tahoe. Built in 1882, this Bed and Breakfast sits on the Truckee River and located in the heart of Old Town. Historic Alpine lodging and river views make the River Street Inn a unique Sierra Mountain Experience. The River Street Inn’s central Truckee location offers easy access to all the region's activities and ski resorts, including Alpine and Nordic Skiing, ice skating, snowshoeing, snowboarding, skiing, and snowmobiling in the winter. For summer time fun, enjoy a day of golfing, hiking, kayaking, swimming, rafting, or water skiing. Boutique shops and excellent restaurants are just a walk across the street from the River Street Inn. Experience the heart of Old Town Truckee at the River Street Inn. No matter what the season, the River Street Inn is a great starting point for a picturesque Lake Tahoe vacation. Perfect for honeymoons, wedding parties, romantic getaways and family reunions.

decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: We opened our second location in Truckee California’s historic downtown in June 2017 to offer another venue to enjoy our beers, along with live music and a creative food menu. Alibi Truckee is a comfortable, flexible space for people to gather and relax. We offer 18+ frequently-rotating Alibi beers on draft, as well as cider, wine, and non-alcoholic options. We offer plenty of to-go beer options: cans, bottles, crowlers and growlers. We also have awesome Alibi merchandise for sale, including gift cards. We are a family-friendly establishment and welcome minors. Dogs are allowed on leash in our outdoor beer garden (weather permitting) but not indoors. We brew small batches of beer here occasionally, often turning the brew day into a homebrewing class. Stay tuned for upcoming dates!

decisions.name: Best Western Plus Hotel Truckee-Tahoe
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: Imagine waking up to crisp mountain air, stepping outside to views of the Sierra Nevada, and knowing your day could include hiking, skiing, or strolling through historic downtown Truckee. At Best Western Plus Truckee Tahoe Hotel, the experience begins the moment you arrive. Whether you're here for a summer festival or a winter escape, this hotel offers a seamless blend of comfort, convenience, and local charm that makes every stay memorable. Located just minutes from Lake Tahoe and top ski resorts like Northstar and Sugar Bowl, the hotel is a gateway to year-round adventure. Guests enjoy a complimentary full hot breakfast featuring burritos and frittatas, perfect fuel before heading out to explore. The heated pool and outdoor hot tub are open year-round, offering relaxation after a day of mountain biking, kayaking, or attending events like Truckee Thursdays or the Barracuda Championship PGA tournament. Inside, each room is thoughtfully designed with plush bedding, free Wi-Fi, a mini-fridge, and a microwave. The property also features a business center, meeting facilities, and coin-operated laundry for extended stays. Tesla chargers are conveniently located next door, and multilingual staff are available 24/7 to assist with your needs.

decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: We love sushi, and we love Truckee, so it was natural for us to combine the two. At Drunken Monkey, we constantly innovate to bring new dishes to life. We push the boundaries of cuisine, with ingredients, techniques and flavor profiles so that your experience is unique whether you're a weeknight regular, or a first time visitor. As innovation expands our access to great ingredients, we have developed relationships with the best suppliers on the West Coast. Our fish is brought in daily from the same suppliers serving some of San Francisco's most revered sushi restaurants. We work with local providers whenever we can to bring world-class fish together with local organic ingredients. The result is an experience that is respectful of the great traditions of sushi but definitively Truckee born.

decisions.decisionId: full-belly-deli-truckee--artisan places to eat drink or--row-140
decisions.name: Full Belly Deli
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: Full Belly Deli was born over a fresh-baked loaf of jalapeño-cheddar bread and two PBRs. Co-owners Eric Barton & Tom Marrin have a few things in common but the strongest bond is their love of a good deli sandwich. Over a couple more fine brews, they laid the groundwork for a place the locals would love and embrace, a dining establishment tourists would seek out on any trip to the Truckee/Tahoe area, and the kind of spot that you couldn't wait to introduce to your friends and family. In December of the 2007, the Town of Truckee changed forever when the boys simultaneously switched on the "Open" and "Pabst" neon signs at Full Belly Deli. Since Day One, the boys and their deli teamsters have been serving up the freshest ingredients combined in ways mere mortals could never even dream (Dorito-encrusted chicken? Biscuits & gravy burrito?) and they'll do it with an approach to customer service that is rare in the area. Most regulars are on a first name basis with the crew but even if it's your first time there, you'll be treated like family.

decisions.description: Welcome to the Tahoe Star Hotel – the heart, soul, and history of Truckee and North Lake Tahoe area a mountain town community. Built in the 1870s by early settler and timberman George Schaffer, the hotel originally housed his family, then later it became lodging for his mill workers. Feel the spirit of the early emigrants, explorers, and lumberjacks with a stay at “Star Hotel ” Ideal for your small groups, solo travelers a romantic retreat or fun-filled family getaway and outdoor adventures. Completely remodeled, the historic eight-room hotel features the modern conveniences you expect in fine lodging, excellent Wi-Fi to power your devices and clear cell phone receptivity, en-suite bathrooms with a shower, a historic charm and modern comforts, filtered water station, complimentary self-serve coffee, tea and snacks and a cozy shared space to unwind after a day of adventure.

decisions.name: Wild Cherries Coffee House
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: Wild Cherries Coffee House has been serving the community since 1998. Kristin Krone, a native Truckee resident and two time Olympian in alpine skiing, along with Gary Talbot, local entrepreneur and fellow coffee connoisseur became acquainted in 1984 at the first local coffee Roastery, Truckee River Coffee Roasters. A shared passion for excellent fresh roasted coffees, European style pastries and a place to meet friends eventually led to a business partnership. In 1995 they began their own coffee roasting venture which ultimately led to Wild Cherries Coffee House in 1998. In 2003 Kristin became sole owner of Wild Cherries continuing the tradition she and Gary had created. The inspiration and mission of Wild Cherries has always been to create an establishment where everyone feels welcome, providing excellent locally roasted coffees and fresh homemade delicious fare. Over 20 years later Wild Cherries continues to be the place to be, a locale for all, where our baristas make you smile, our bustling kitchen makes you happy and the people you meet here will make your day.

decisions.decisionId: el-avagve-taqueria-auburn--artisan places to eat drink or--row-188
decisions.name: El Avagve Taqueria
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: Jalisco is the home town for “El Agave Taqueria” owners, a town that is highly popular for its hard liquor “Tequila”, witch comes from the blue agave plant represented in the logo of this restaurant. Tequila is a type of hard liquor which comes from a small region of Jalisco and which is made primarily from the blue agave plant. It is the most famous type of mezcal produced in Mexico, and the only mezcal which is produced industrially with strict standards. It is named after a small town northwest of the city of Guadalajara in the center of where it is produced and the native region of the blue agave. The plant was used in pre-Hispanic times to make a ceremonial drink. The Spanish used the sweet heart of the mature plant, called a piña (literally pineapple) to create a fermented and distilled beverage. The first person to have official permission to make and sell the liquor was José Antonio Cuervo in 1758. In 1888, the first license to export was given to the Sauza family.

Join us at COMO, where every meal is a celebration of heritage and community.

decisions.name: Front Street Station Pizza
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: Since 2004, Front Street Station Pizza Company has been a Tahoe City tradition for both locals and visitors.The Front Street family takes exceptional pride in serving some of Lake Tahoe area's very best, handcrafted food. Front Street Station's warm and welcoming log cabin structure on the banks of the Truckee River in Tahoe City, offer both a relaxed atmosphere and a cozy neighborhood feel. Everyone LOVES the New York-style pizza and craves the authentic Mexican specialties and mouth-watering sandwiches.

decisions.decisionId: donner-creek-brewing-truckee--artisan places to eat drink or--row-200
decisions.name: Donner Creek Brewing
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: We specialize in small batch, craft brew, good times & delicious grilled cheese panini-pressed sandwiches. We are family-and-dog-friendly, offer free pool with weekly tournaments on Monday nights and live music most Friday nights. We LOVE living & playing in the beautiful Truckee outdoors & live for sharing our adventurous stoke with all of our guests! Come by & say "Hi" - it won't take long before you'll know why people call us the "Cheers of Truckee" :)

decisions.decisionId: truckee-tavern-truckee--artisan places to eat drink or--row-201
decisions.name: Truckee Tavern
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: We believe a great dining experience must not only be made with fresh quality ingredients and be expertly prepared but it must invoke emotion and memory. Truckee Tavern’s food and beverage offerings include the freshest, highest quality local ingredients, farm raised, hormone free meats and locally grown produce to give a sense of place. Menu items showcase the incredible diversity of food available in California. The menus changes continually to reflect the seasons, changing tastes and availability to give the feel of moment. Retaining crowd favorites with new and exciting interpretations invokes anticipation in our regulars. These offerings are paired expertly with the finest wine, spirit and beer selections. Truckee Tavern’s spirit program draws from Truckee’s rich history of bootlegging during prohibition and focus on artisanal small batch gin and whiskey, the two main distillation products from Truckee during that era. This is complemented by a thoughtfully chosen selection of fine rum, vodka, brandy, tequila, mescal, liquors and cordials, all complimenting the overall experience.

decisions.decisionId: coffeebar-bakery-truckee--artisan places to eat drink or--row-202
decisions.name: Coffeebar Bakery
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: Three locations in North Lake Tahoe! Don't let the name fool you - Coffeebar is more than just your average coffee shop. This radically-inclusive Italian Cafe - as they describe themselves - has the makings for breakfast, lunch, dinner and great conversation. Coffebar is known for holding strong beleifs in local, high quality food and sustainable business practices. Born out of an old tin garage and transformed into a modern style cafe, Coffeebar has everything from lattes and pastries to wine and quinoa bowls. Immerse yourself in authenicity at this attraction that features local art and culture.

decisions.name: Darkhorse Coffee Roasters Truckee
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: A place for crunchy dirtbags, local classics, academics, poets, philosophers, artists, angels, devils, mirrors, mystics, skeptics, rebels, those with money, those without, those lost, those found, leave your judgement at the door. Give a stranger a compliment. Try your coffee with no sweetener. Give the Dark Side a try. Take a Ride.

decisions.decisionId: drink-coffee-do-stuff-truckee--artisan places to eat drink or--row-204
decisions.name: Drink Coffee Do Stuff
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: DRINK COFFEE DO STUFF is a specialty coffee company built with the belief that extraordinary coffee leads to an extraordinary life. The mantra began in the Swiss Alps in 2012 during founder Nick Visconti’s pro snowboard days and continues today at our Lake Tahoe headquarters. As specialty coffees grow at elevations similar to that of our roastery, all our coffees are sourced from the mountains and roasted in the mountains. The hallmark of our coffee roasting style is maximizing sweetness and revealing origin characteristic. High altitude roasting at 6,000ft in Lake Tahoe provides us unique advantages to achieve this consistently making all our coffees more sweet, less bitter.

decisions.description: Venture out into nature and experience one of the most unique stays in our mountain lodge. With cozy accommodations and amenities to keep you busy, our lodge is the perfect place to stay for any traveler looking to get out into nature. decisions.name: The Truckee Food Shop
decisions.category: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
decisions.description: High in the Sierras, you will find The Truckee Food Shop, a curated deli and market serving fresh food all year-round. Our food is prepared daily and sourced from organic farms, area ranchers, fishermen, and local co-ops. We offer a full take-out menu made from the seasonal bounty of California and Nevada ingredients. We prepare delicious family-style “oven-ready” meals for any occasion. In addition to our regular menu, we offer weekly specials, made-to-order sandwiches, and farm-to-fork fare. Please ask about our vegetarian and gluten-free options. We also offer catering for private dinners and special events.

decisions.name: Paul Emery Presents
decisions.description: Mr. Emery is a professional musician, producer and audio engineer (approx. 35 years). From 1998 to 2000, he contracted with Steve Lavere to restore vintage blues and jazz recordings. He has his own audio studio and audio production equipment (used for live sound, archiving, audio restoration, album/cd production and audio editing). He also owns a van used for equipment transport. Current clients, in addition to his own productions through Paul Emery Presents, include NC Chamber of Commerce, The Center for the Arts, Strings Concerts, KVMR, Nevada Theatre, Miners Foundry, Auburn Park and Recreation District, Alasdair Fraser, North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center, numerous bands, Legacy Productions, Oddfellows, GV Elks and others. In 2012, Emery was awarded the prestigious Dr. Leland and Sally Lewis Performing Arts award by the Nevada City Chamber of Commerce for his contribution and support of the performing arts in Nevada County.

decisions.name: 4th of July Celebration Fireworks

decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Celtic Festival
decisions.description: First founded in 1996, the KVMR Celtic Festival includes eleven performance areas including five musical and six demonstration stages and a weekend long youth arts instruction that has included Celtic music, magic, dance and songwriting.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Cornish Christmas
decisions.description: Cornish Christmas started in 1967 as a way to preserve Grass Valley’s Cornish heritage and holiday traditions. Mill and West Main Streets are closed to motorized traffic and filled with the sights and sounds of an old fashioned Christmas; carolers, jugglers, musicians, magicians, the Grass Valley Cornish Carol Choir, Tommyknocker Cloggers and of course, Santa Claus. Enjoy delicious food and drink while perusing handmade arts and crafts from artists throughout California.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Country Christmas Craft Fair
decisions.description: Grass Valley hosts a major Country Christmas Faire at the Nevada County Fairgrounds with 100+ artisans, Santa, bonfire, music, and crafts.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Cruzin' in the Pines Car Show
decisions.description: One of the best classic car shows in Northern California.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Draft Horse Classic
decisions.description: The Classic has grown to become the premier Draft Horse show in the western United States, and features six majestic performances, breathtaking competitions and entertainment in between classes.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Earth's Treasures Gem & Mineral Show
decisions.description: Check out the gem and mineral collections, visit the booths of the numerous vendors, bid on minerals and rocks at a silent auction, watch local artisans at work, and see the demonstrations. This show is a great place to meet other enthusiasts, buy supplies, and experience everything the rock and mineral hobby has to offer.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Fall Colors Open Studio Tour (host:Center for the Arts)
decisions.description: The Center for the Arts, with support from the Nevada County Arts Council, presents the Fall Colors Open Studio Tour. Over 70 local artists welcome the public to 47 studios and art galleries during a free tour spanning two weekends in October.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Fathers Day Bluegrass Festival
decisions.description: California Bluegrass Association presents a legendary bluegrass, old time, and gospel music festival over four days in June.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Foothills Celebration
decisions.description: The Foothills Celebration is just the therapy you’ll need for a late winter weekend. The wineries and restaurants are all paired with local downtown Grass Valley merchants who open their stores for this unique wine and food tasting affair.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Grass Valley Car Show
decisions.description: Travel back in time and experience American nostalgia at its best. See approximately 300 of the finest custom, vintage and antique cars and trucks in all of Northern California; in a city that honors its historic past and embraces its wonderful future: Historic Downtown Grass Valley.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Grass Valley Farmers Market
decisions.description: The Grass Valley Thursday Night Markets are a wonderful community event that includes a certified Farmer’s Market and a whole lot more including arts, crafts, food, and the ever popular FREE Summer Concert Series.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Grass Valley Old West Antique Show
decisions.description: The annual Old West Antiques Show provides the opportunity to view and purchase world class merchandise from a legendary list of dealers in all collecting categories, including museum quality Antique Firearms, Native American items, Western & Cowboy Memorabilia, Advertising, Coin Operated & Gambling, Saloon & General Store, Paintings, Bronzes, Lamps, Gas & Oil, Jewelry, Furniture and more!
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Holidays at the Empire Mine
decisions.description: Celebrate the holidays early 1900s’ style at Holidays at Empire Mine. With old fashioned decorations, lavish period costumes, and festive live music, Empire Cottage will be brimming with good cheer. Docents will share insights about Empire’s glory days when it ranked as one of the most prosperous hard-rock gold mines in North American history
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Miners Picnic
decisions.description: The annual Miners Picnic is a summer celebration of our colorful, gold mining history. The first Miners Picnic was held in 1895 to raise money for miners’ widows and orphans, as well as injured and out of work miners. Today, it’s an action packed day of fun, food, gold history, and lasting memories.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Music in the Mountains Summer Fest
decisions.description: For more than 37 years, Music in the Mountains SummerFest has been Northern California’s premier summer concert series, featuring exciting outdoor performances and sophisticated chamber concerts, demonstrating that classical music, performed by a world class orchestra and chorus, can transform lives and communities.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada County Certified Growers Market
decisions.description: Nevada County's original certified market, bringing growers and the community together since 1980.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada County Fair
decisions.description: Five days of excitement and fun for Fair goers of all ages. Enjoy live entertainment, delicious food, carnival rides, animals, and exhibits in a community friendly environment and at affordable family prices!
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Sierra BrewFest
decisions.description: A unique, unlimited microbrew tasting experience. Sample over 60 microbrews and a no host wine bar for those who would prefer wine over beer! This year features even more delicious food from some of the best local food trucks and restaurants.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Sierra Poetry Festival
decisions.description: In addition to its mainstage event, Sierra Poetry Festival marks National Poetry Month from the rolling foothills of California’s Gold Country to the rugged High Sierra, bringing our rich literary community together to celebrate the spoken word and reach out to brand new audiences in fresh ways. Readings, workshops, music, an activity fair for all ages and youth performances will be preceded by a month of pre festival pop-up poetry events.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: St. Piran's Day Festival
decisions.description: Every year Grass Valley captures some fun with its annual St. Piran’s Day pasty tossing contest. The rain or shine event, which celebrates the Cornish immigrants who settled around the gold mines, is fun for adults, children and especially dogs.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Strawberry Music Festival
decisions.description: The Strawberry Music Festival presents the gold standard in main stage entertainment, intimate side stage performances, top notch children’s programming, workshops and activities for the whole family, and some of the nicest people you will ever meet. Programs like the Breakfast Club and the Band Scramble, live broadcasts by Hog Ranch Radio, and Strawberry’s legendary camp jam culture are what makes this your festival.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Thursday Night Markets
decisions.description: Every Thursday evening, our charming streets come alive with an eclectic mix of artisan vendors, farm-fresh produce, sizzling street food, and toe-tapping live music.Wander through local maker booths, sip something cool, and catch up with friends old and new. From handmade crafts and gourmet treats to face painting and family fun, there’s something for everyone. Whether you’re a long-time local or a first-time visitor, this is summer in Grass Valley at its best—vibrant, friendly, and unforgettable.

The parade through the downtown historic district includes marching bands, floats, antique autos, equestrians, politicians and the perennial crowd favorites the Famous Marching Presidents of Nevada City.

decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Mardis Gras Parade
decisions.description: The 27th anniversary of Nevada City’s annual Mardi Gras Celebration is set to happen on Sunday, February 17, 2019. The annual event, hosted by the Nevada City Chamber of Commerce, features a colorful & festive parade & street faire throughout the streets of historic downtown Nevada City.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.description: NCFF screens almost 100 films, including comedies, dramas, documentaries, animation, and international shorts, in a variety of historic locations in downtown Nevada City. By combining creative programming of short and full length films, enthusiastic audiences and a truly festive environment, NCFF has become known as one of the most innovative, fun and artist friendly events on the film festival circuit.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada City Summer Nights
decisions.description: The Summer Nights in Nevada City celebration, a street festival of art and music, has been an annual tradition in this quaint, scenic, historic (and sometimes quirky) community, for close to 30 years. This free event takes place on three Wednesday evenings in July, when Nevada City’s landmark historic district is closed to motorized traffic and filled with arts, crafts, classic cars, food, drink and music. Leading Sierra foothills musicians perform on outdoor stages throughout the downtown area.

decisions.name: Eric Rood Administration Center Lobby
decisions.description: Public art must always strive to be physically and freely accessible. It can be for simple enjoyment or carry many meanings. It can be set in stone, span a wall, be ephemeral or permanent. It can be fantastic or representational; tell a story, lend civic pride, and remind us that though we may be unique in our identities, we are one. Our program is about providing a thoughtful space of belonging for all residents of Nevada County, expressing community values, heightening our awareness, and helping us question our assumptions. Art in Public Spaces acknowledges both our California Cultural Districts, and communities that populate our diverse landscapes. It is for everyone, a form of collective community expression, reflecting our society and opening our civic spaces.

decisions.name: Neighborhood Center of the Arts
decisions.description: NCA’s roots in Nevada County go back to 1966, when the Community Workshop was founded as a jobs training center for adults with developmental disabilities. Internationally acclaimed ceramicist, Gillian Hodge began an art program at the Community Workshop in 1973. Ten years later, the art program ended due to government cutbacks. In 1984, Hodge and supporters within the community founded the Neighborhood Center of the Arts, a new program to provide arts based training and education. The first location was in a small house in downtown Grass Valley. Over the next ten years, NCA moved several times to accommodate the expanding program. In 1992 the Nevada County Community Workshop/Community Achievement Center merged with Neighborhood Center of the Arts. NCA introduced the Miguel Quesada Program–our 1:4 Community program–to serve a wider group of people with disabilities. We’ve been located at the Litton Building since 1995. Here, we have been able to expand within this facility as our program grows.

decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Grass Roots Antique Tool Museum
decisions.description: Our specialty is one-of-a-kind custom installations designed virtually from photos of your home, office or business.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Kitkitdizzi
decisions.description: It's pronounced: kit-kid-dizzy, but without heavily spacing or over annunciating those four syllables. It's a native plant. Bear Clover, Mountain Misery or Kitkitdizze, as the Miwok referred to it, is endemic to the western side of the Sierra Nevada. If you've hiked in these woods and haven't noticed it underfoot, it's resinous leaves adhering to your pants and shoes, you've most certainly taken in the scent of this highly aromatic little fernlike plant carpeting the forest floor. It is essential to the health of the forests in these parts and for many of us, is the smell of home.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: LeeAnn Brook Gallery
decisions.description: Opened in July 2015, LeeAnn Brook Fine Art is one of the first artist-owned gallery and on site studio in Nevada County, California. Located at 231 Broad St, in the heart of historic downtown Nevada City, the gallery also incorporates the working studio of LeeAnn Brook, where visitors can see paintings in progress and discuss the art with the artist firsthand.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Lilly Vigil Gallery
decisions.description: Lilly Vigil Gallery is located within the heart of the Sierra Nevada in beautiful downtown Nevada City, California. Since 1985, Vigil's is recognized for unparalleled quality of artistic and cultural treasures, focusing on fine Native American art. The gallery also specializes in originals, graphics, sculptors and exquisite jewelry from local, regional, and national artists.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum
decisions.description: Located in Nevada City, California, the Nevada County Narrow Gauge Railroad and Transportation Museum is dedicated to the preservation of local transportation history and artifacts from the narrow gauge railroad era. Visitors are offered a docent led historical tour of the museum, rail yard, and restoration shop. There is no admission charge.

decisions.description: The Searls Historical Library is the archive of the Nevada County Historical Society. Established in 1973, the Searls Library has become one of the premier libraries of materials on the area's history, people and mines which contributed to the development of this county.

decisions.name: Utopian Stone
decisions.description: Nestled in the Sierra Nevada Foothills, Utopian Stone was founded in 1974 by G. Patrick Dyer and wife, Diane. We employ four master goldsmiths, plus a fifth, Dean Dussell, who works from his studio in Colorado. At Utopian Stone we specialize in high quality one of a kind work and customer commissions. Our favorite thing is to design for clients who have no idea what they want. As we do not reproduce our one of a kind creations, our customers challenge our designers who may want something similar to a one of a kind piece, but with its own unique personal flair

decisions.description: The Western SkiSport Museum was founded in 1969 by the Auburn Ski Club. Working with ski writer and historian William B. Berry, the Museum was developed as an exhibition of Western North American ski history. Today the Museum continues on a mission to collect, preserve and exhibit the history of winter ski sports in the western United States.

Atelier strives to be the creative hub of the local art community by hosting workshops taught by the area’s most sought after artists, illustrators, designers, weavers, knitters, painters and more who share the vision to create always and inspire all.

decisions.description: The Emigrant Trail Museum contains exhibits about the cultural history of the area, including local Native Americans, the Donner Party, and building of the First Transcontinental Railroad.

Gallery 5830’ also facilitates artist/ client commissions that emphasize a collaborative vision, site-specific designs and refined old-world craftsmanship.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Joseph Research Cabin
decisions.description: Truckee Donner Historical Society was founded July 24, 1968 to preserve the history of the Truckee Donner area. Besides maintaining the historic Joseph Research Cabin (the Gateway Cabin), the Society is also responsible for operating the Old Jail Museum which was built in 1875. The Society maintains the Community Memorial Garden including the Memorial Arch behind the Old Jail Museum, the Donner Cross, and Schaffer Cemetery.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Lorien Powers Studio Jewelry
decisions.description: Lorien Powers is an artist who specializes in creating industrial and contemporary style jewelry. Lorien prefers to work with gold, silver, natural gems, and minerals. Known for the decorative features of her work, she employs a wide variety of techniques, including roller printing, cold connecting, riveting, fabricating and forging. She draws inspiration for her jewelry from many different sources, some from nature, some from music (Phish in particular), some from industrial environments.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Mountain Arts Collective
decisions.description: Located at the Train Depot in historic Downtown Truckee, Mountain Arts Collective began in 2017, with three local artists who wanted to showcase quality art by individuals closely connected to the community. Our goal is to create a welcoming space that will attract locals and visitors with high end statement pieces, more affordable arts & gifts, and everything in between.

decisions.name: Old Jail Museum
decisions.description: Truckee’s historic jail museum is one of only a few surviving 19th-century jailhouses of its kind in the West and one of the few remaining original buildings in Truckee. The old Bastille was used continuously from 1875 until May 1964, and ably withstood the many fires that swept the town in its early days. Although jails are usually sturdy, Truckee’s is a virtual strongbox. The original building consisted of just the lower level, constructed of native stone. The walls are 32-inches thick at the lower level, with no windows unless one counts the small vents for each cell, which are set with irregular rows of two-inch steel bars. The ceilings are plate steel, insulated with dirt, and lined with narrow gauge railroad tracks. All doors are riveted steel, weighing an estimated 200 pounds each.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Riverside Studios
decisions.description: Riverside Studios mission is to cultivate artistic growth in the Truckee Community while handcrafting one of a kind artistic goods, creating a unique shopping experience for both locals and tourists alike. decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Truckee Railroad Museum
decisions.description: Truckee Railroad Museum is located next to the historic railroad station in downtown Truckee. This adds historical significance to the area, as the caboose is from the Southern Pacific Railroad and was used in the area. Paint for the caboose matches the original SP paint scheme. Inside the museum are stories, pictures, recreations, and railroad artifacts depicting the impact of railroads in the formation and development of Truckee. The First Transcontinental Railroad, Logging Railroads, and Tourism by Rail all played an important part in Truckee’s history.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Truckee Tahoe Airport District (Art at the Airport)
decisions.description: Since 1958, the Truckee Tahoe Airport District has been providing a high quality, safe facility for pilots, visitors and the community. Nestled in the Sierra Nevada mountain range at an elevation of 5,900 ft., twelve miles from Lake Tahoe, we promise one of the most scenic flight and visiting experiences in the world. We are proud of our pilot safety and community programs, state of the art energy saving facilities and top notch staff and leadership. We are committed to offering the best small, mountain airport in the Country.

We proudly represent a curated roster of local, regional, and international mid-career and established artists whose work resonates with discerning collectors, interior designers, and homeowners seeking refined, statement-making pieces. Exhibitions rotate monthly, with each new show opening during the Truckee Art Walk, held on the first Saturday of every month from 4–7pm. decisions.name: Bear Yuba Land Trust
decisions.description: Bear Yuba Land Trust is a private, non profit, membership supported organization that works to promote voluntary conservation of the region’s natural, historical and agricultural legacy. The Land Trust provides a lasting community heritage by protecting and enhancing meadows, watersheds, forests, farms and ranches, trails and parks. BYLT exists to create a balance between nature and the needs of people like you who make a life and a livelihood here. Our mission is to enrich your deep community connection with the land today, tomorrow, and forever.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: City of Grass Valley Historical Commission
decisions.description: It is the intent of the City to encourage the preservation of the City's historical and architecturally significant resources by the formation of a Historical Commission which has the duty and function to identify and designate historical properties within the City and to honor those property owners who have maintained or restored these historical properties.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Daughters of the American Revolution – Capt. John Oldham Chapter
decisions.description: The DAR, founded in 1890 and headquartered in Washington, DC is a non profit, non-political, volunteer women's service organization dedicated to promoting patriotism, preserving American history, and securing America's future through better education for children.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Historic Mount St. Mary’s Preservation Committee
decisions.description: St. Joseph's Cultural Center is a nonprofit organization dedicated to preserving a historic landmark for cultural activities in the community. It is owned and operated by the Historic Mount. St. Mary's Preservation committee.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada County Gem & Mineral Society
decisions.description: The Nevada County Gem and Mineral Society is a non-profit organization that has the following objectives: To promote the study of mineralogy, geology, and fossils. To encourage the collection of minerals and gems. To foster the study and practice of the lapidary arts. To provide field trips to mineral localities. To promote good fellowship, education and recreation.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: North Star Historic Conservancy
decisions.description: The Conservancy is working to re establish the unique, historic North Star House and grounds into a premier cultural event center. It is committed to maintaining the property for the public by promoting tours, events educational activities and the arts.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Gold Country Celtic Society
decisions.description: The Gold Country Celtic Society was organized to celebrate the culture of these proud people, many of whom, especially the Cornish and Welsh, worked the hard rock mines and made our Nevada City Grass Valley area famous. The Society wishes to perpetuate Celtic traditions of music, dance,language, and costume.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada Cemetery District
decisions.description: The Nevada Cemetery District is an independent special district charged with providing respectful and cost effective interment services appropriate for the diversity of local conditions and circumstances. It is responsible for the improvement, expansion and operation of public cemeteries in Western Nevada County, including historic cemeteries established by fraternal, pioneer, religious, social and other organizations
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada City Rancheria of the Nisenan Tribe
decisions.description: The Nevada City Rancheria was created from an executive order by President Woodrow Wilson in 1913 from a land allotment obtained by Chief Charlie Cully in 1887. Cully's land on Cement Hill became Federal Trust Land and the Tribal government became a federally recognized entity. The Nevada City Rancheria was one of 48 Rancherias illegally terminated in the 1950s and 1960s by the California Rancheria Termination Acts. Most have been restored; the Nevada City Nisenan Rancheria has not. Yet.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada County Cultural Preservation Trust
decisions.description: The Miners Foundry Cultural Center is a living historic treasure serving Nevada County as a cultural arts and community events center. More than a venue, the nonprofit’s mission is to preserve, enhance and utilize the historic Miners Foundry for cultural, educational and social activities in Nevada County. It is a symbol of California’s rich historical heritage, providing an educational and social bridge from our past to the present and future.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada County Historical Society
decisions.description: The Nevada County Historical Society had its beginning in the fall of 1944 after being inspired by Dr. Robert E. Burns, President of the College of the Pacific. The objective of the new organization was to preserve every source of Nevada County history. Monthly programs were planned to entertain and educate members, and the committee chairmen and groups formed the foundation of the society by collecting, displaying, and preserving the memorabilia of the county.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Nevada Theatre Commission
decisions.description: The Nevada Theatre Commission is a group of volunteers who are dedicated to making sure the Nevada Theatre continues to be available and usable to our community for years to come. The Nevada Theatre is owned and maintained by the the Commission which is a non profit 501(c)(3) organization.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Ngakmang Foundation
decisions.description: To preserve the spiritual, medical, and cultural traditions of the Himalayan and Tibetan region.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: South Yuba River Citizens League
decisions.description: SYRCL unites the community to protect and restore the Yuba River. Motivated by our love for this watershed, we advocate powerfully, engage in active stewardship, educate the public, and inspire activism from the Sierra to the sea.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: The Sierra Fund
decisions.description: The Sierra Fund’s mission is to restore ecosystem and community resiliency in the Sierra Nevada.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: California Heritage Indigenous Research Project
decisions.description: CALIFORNIA HERITAGE: INDIGENOUS RESEARCH PROJECT (CHIRP) was created to research, document, preserve, and protect California Indigenous Nisenan culture. CHIRP continues to support the Nisenan in their quest to reestablish themselves as the Indigenous people in the foothills where their families have resided for thousands of years. CHIRP's area of influence is mainly the counties of Sierra, Yuba, Sutter, Nevada, Placer and Butte.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Famous Marching Presidents Inc.
decisions.description: The Famous Marching Presidents organization honors the U.S. Constitution in the involvement of the Nevada City Constitution Day Parade and other related public celebrations.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Donner Summit Historical Society
decisions.description: Donner Summit Historical Society is dedicated to telling the story of the most important historical square mile in California and maybe the entire Western U.S. We operate a museum on the Summit as well as the 20 Mile Museum along Old Highway 40.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: C A T T Community Project
decisions.description: CATT Community Project is a 501c(3) non profit, charitable organization established in October 1999 as a sponsored arm of the Contractors Association of Truckee Tahoe. CATT Community Project is governed by a 10 member Board of Directors which meets monthly. CATT Community Project provides design and construction assistance to other non profits and governmental agencies as a way of giving back to the community.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Mountain Area Preservation Foundation
decisions.description: To preserve the Truckee Tahoe region’s mountain character and natural environment for present and future generations.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Truckee Donner Historical Society
decisions.description: Truckee Donner Historical Society was founded July 24, 1968 to preserve the history of the Truckee Donner area. Besides maintaining the historic Joseph Research Cabin (the Gateway Cabin), the Society is also responsible for operating the Old Jail Museum which was built in 1875. The Society maintains the Community Memorial Garden including the Memorial Arch behind the Old Jail Museum, the Donner Cross, and Schaffer Cemetery.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Truckee Donner Horsemen
decisions.description: Truckee Donner Horsemen is a non-profit organization of people who love horses. We are based in Truckee, CA in the high Sierra mountains. Our Mission is to Promote Horsemanship and Preserve the McIver Arena
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Truckee Donner Railroad Society
decisions.description: The mission of the Truckee Donner Railroad Society is to perpetuate the study and advancement of knowledge of working railroads in the Truckee Donner area with the goal of building a railroad museum to preserve and share what we have learned.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Old Truckee Jail Museum
decisions.description: As volunteers for this non profit 501(c)(3) organization, we are stewards of a number of unique physical assets (see below). The TDHS invites you to join with us in the preservation and maintenance of these irreplaceable historical venues, and at the same time have lots of fun in the process discovering our Truckee, California heritage. Our MISSION is to discover, procure and preserve whatever may relate to the aboriginal, natural, civil, literary, and ecclesiastical history of the Town of Truckee and surrounding area. Our VISION is to preserve and protect the history of Truckee. We are a small group of very dedicated volunteers who love Truckee and its rich history, and who continue to serve in whatever capacities are required to continue our mission.
decisions.reviewNotes: US Census Geocoder result accepted as a free, interpolated street-address coordinate; not storefront/building certainty.

decisions.name: Museum of Truckee History
decisions.description: The Museum of Truckee History is a non-profit collaboration between the Truckee-Donner Historical Society and the Truckee Donner Railroad Society. The museum is staffed and supported entirely by volunteers from the community. The mission of the museum is to bring Truckee’s history alive by illustrating the stories of its major industries through the display of artifacts and exhibits, creation of documentary films, and access to lectures, educational programs, and research facilities.
decisions.reviewNotes: US Census Geocoder result accepted as a free, interpolated street-address coordinate; not storefront/building certainty.

decisions.name: Truckee Donner Land Trust
decisions.description: Since 1990, the Land Trust has not only preserved more than 40,000 acres of open space, but has also built trails, manages a campground, and has created numerous opportunities for the public to access and enjoy these lands.

decisions.name: The Martis Fund
decisions.description: The Martis Fund was created in 2006 by conservationists and landowners committed to the long-term future of Martis Valley. The Fund supports programs to conserve open space; manage and restore habitat and forest lands; and promote opportunities for workforce housing and related community purposes in the Martis Valley Region. We fulfill our mission primarily through grants to qualifying 501 (C) (3) non-profit organizations active in the region. Funding for our grant programs comes from private community benefit fees (transfer fees). The Fund’s primary role is managing and dispersing financial resources raised in connection with real estate sales (transfer fees) at Martis Camp. We connect non-profit organizations to financial resources to complete projects serving our mission.

decisions.name: Fountain sculpture at Pioneer Park
decisions.description: Design: Francis Lloyd, Sculptor: Norman Grag. The fountain was installed in 1976 with funds from the John J. Looser estate. The fountain was designed by architect Francis Lloyd and the metal sculpture was created by artist Norman Grag. The fountain was placed in the middle of an existing city street that ran through the park. The placement of the fountain resulted in an intentional redirection of the road.

decisions.name: Mural at Pioneer Park swimming pool
decisions.description: Artist: Jordan Mal did the project in 2016 as her Senior Project. Mural on the wall at the swimming pool -- serves as a painting and safety signage. The wave on the wall matches the depth of the swimming pool, so if a child stands by the wall and the wave is over their head, they know that if they walk straight from that spot into the poolду_the water will be over their head.

decisions.name: Robinson Plaza -- Mining sculptures: Pelton Water Wheel and Five Stamp Mill

Donated to Nevada City by Bill Magers. Dedicated July 4, 1982

decisions.description: Artist: Bernie LaForest of Striker Forge. ''DeRailed'' which is part of the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project by TPAC, is made of repurposed rail steel. decisions.name: Locomotion"" bike sculpture
decisions.description: Artist: Fred Besch. ''Locomotion'' was created by local kinetic artist Fred Besch. The giant bike sculpture can be found in the plaza west of the historic Flying A building. The sculpture represents residents' love of biking. The art shares plaza space with a giant chess board that was created and donated by Truckee Roundhouse Makerspace.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Martis Sculpture"" - front of Martis Outlook professional building.
decisions.description: Artist: Anton Standteiner, Mountain Forge. ''Martis Sculpture'' was created by artist Anton Standteiner of The Mountain Forge and commissioned by the Town of Truckee.The flow and style of the sculpture honors the Native American people who once inhabited Martis Valley. It stands in front of the Martis Outlook professional building on the corner of Soaring Way and Truckee Tahoe Airport Way.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Mountain Flowers"" sculpture
decisions.description: Artist: Troy Corliss, Gallery 5830'. Mountain Flowers sculpture stands 16-18 feet high in the roundabout across from the Truckee Donner Community Center on Donner Pass Rd.. The sculpture was a gift to the Town of Truckee from artist Troy Corliss of Gallery 5830'. Other contributors, in services or supplies, were The Mountain Forge, JMC Industrial Finishing, Huldrege & Kull, and Perennial Landscape & Nursery.

decisions.name: The Feather"" bike rack project.
decisions.description: Artist: Lauren Bello Okerman. ''The Feather'' is part of the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project by TPAC. It was designed by Lauren Bello Okerman and is made of powder coated steel. The art serves as a bike rack in front of the Truckee Donner Recreation & Park District. This sculpture was sponsored by the Queen of Hearts Women's Fund.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Animal Tracks"" -- bike rack project. At Tahoe Forest Hospital.
decisions.description: Artist: Kevin Kremler. ''Animal Tracks'' is a painted steel bike rack sculpture featuring paw prints of local animals. It was made by artist Kevin Kremler and is part of the TPAC Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project. It was commissioned by Jae Gustafson for Tahoe Forest Hospital.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Community As Ecosystem""
decisions.description: Artists: Sara L. Smith, Toni Standteiner/Mountain Forge. Commissioned by the TRDRPD, 'думCommunity As Ecosystem" main mural image measures more than 11 ft. high by 16 ft. wide. The piece celebrates Truckee's new Aquatic Center donors. The artists represent the concept of joyful balance noting each donor (no matter level of contribution) is a vital part of the overall effort. The mural speaks to the wider concept of community as an ecosystem, and reminds us that we are all essential. The artists note: "The mural is painted using sepia tones to tie us back to our past, and is painted upon a wall created with reclaimed Lake Tahoe pier wood, creating a tangible tie to our physical past and present. It depicts a stylized image of Martis Valleyду»s diverse natural environment, and donors are represented by a combination of cut and manipulated animal shapes and forged iron environmental elements, such as mountain peaks, cattails and wave forms in the creek. On a separate panel each and every donorду»s name has been hand lettered, grouped next to its corresponding donor category and animal shape.дуќ In addition two additional small panels were created to specifically recognize the individuals who helped shepherd the project to fruition, and well as the organizations that contributed multi-million dollar grant funds.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.

decisions.name: Aspen Grove"" in Radiation Lobby at Tahoe Forest Cancer Center
decisions.description: Artist: Troy Corliss. The colorful array of glass leaves in the Troy Corliss думAspen Groveдуќ welcomes patients and visitors to the Radiation Therapy waiting space. Fabricated in steel and kiln fused glass, the artwork integrates an experience of the natural world with interior architecture. The gentle curves of the tree trunks and the subtle patterning and warm colors of the glass leaves creates a three dimensional design that is both intriguing and calming. The Grove can be experienced as a powerful whole at first sight or explored with interest with repeated visits.
decisions.reviewNotes: Diana Workbook supplied valid Nevada County coordinates.
